title: First FIFA World Cup winner
fact: Uruguay won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930 by defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final in Montevideo.

title: Youngest World Cup final scorer
fact: Pele scored twice in the 1958 FIFA World Cup final and remains the youngest player to score in a World Cup final at age 17.

title: Historic World Cup semifinal
fact: Germany defeated Brazil 7-1 in the 2014 FIFA World Cup semifinal in Belo Horizonte.

title: First Cricket World Cup winner
fact: West Indies won the first men's Cricket World Cup in 1975, defeating Australia in the final at Lord's.

title: India's first ODI World Cup title
fact: India won its first men's ODI Cricket World Cup in 1983 by defeating West Indies at Lord's.

title: First ICC Men's T20 World Cup winner
fact: India won the inaugural ICC Men's T20 World Cup in 2007 by defeating Pakistan in the final.

title: Dream Team Olympic gold
fact: The 1992 United States men's basketball team, known as the Dream Team, won Olympic gold in Barcelona.

title: Historic NBA Finals comeback
fact: The Cleveland Cavaliers won the 2016 NBA Finals after trailing 3-1, the first such comeback in NBA Finals history.

title: Early three-point line usage
fact: The ABA used the three-point line before the NBA; the NBA officially adopted the three-point line in the 1979-80 season.

title: Start of the Open Era
fact: The Open Era in tennis began in 1968, allowing professional and amateur players to compete together in Grand Slam events.

title: Only singles Golden Slam
fact: Steffi Graf won all four Grand Slam singles titles and Olympic gold in 1988, completing the only Golden Slam in singles tennis.

title: Federer breaks major record
fact: Roger Federer won his 15th Grand Slam singles title at Wimbledon in 2009, surpassing Pete Sampras's previous men's record.

title: First modern Olympic Games
fact: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens in 1896.

title: Jesse Owens in Berlin
fact: Jesse Owens won four gold medals at the 1936 Berlin Olympics.

title: Most gold medals in one Olympics
fact: Michael Phelps won eight gold medals at the 2008 Beijing Olympics, the most by any athlete at a single Olympic Games.

title: First F1 World Drivers' Champion
fact: Giuseppe Farina won the inaugural Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship in 1950.

title: Schumacher's 7th title
fact: Michael Schumacher secured his seventh Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship in 2004.

title: Verstappen's first title
fact: Max Verstappen won his first Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship in 2021.

title: Robinson breaks MLB color barrier
fact: Jackie Robinson debuted for the Brooklyn Dodgers in 1947, breaking Major League Baseball's modern color barrier.

title: Maris's single-season home run mark
fact: Roger Maris hit 61 home runs in the 1961 MLB season, setting a new American League single-season record at the time.

title: Consecutive games record
fact: Cal Ripken Jr. surpassed Lou Gehrig's record by playing his 2,131st consecutive MLB game in 1995.

title: First sub-four-minute mile
fact: Roger Bannister became the first person to run a mile in under four minutes in 1954.

title: Bolt's 100m world record
fact: Usain Bolt set the men's 100m world record of 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin.

title: Women's 100m record
fact: Florence Griffith-Joyner set the women's 100m world record of 10.49 seconds in 1988.
